steps.title: Track the exceptional moon
steps.content: The text says 20 moons were confirmed. All but one are thought to be remnants of earlier bodies that broke apart. The one exceptional moon orbits in the same direction as Saturn’s spin, so it has a prograde orbit, and its unusual eccentricity may suggest a different origin.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported details
steps.content: Choice A says the exceptional moon likely has the same origin as the retrograde moons, but the text says it may have a different origin. Choice B says it is neither prograde nor retrograde, but it orbits in the direction Saturn spins. Choice C invents a collision as the reason for its orbit. The supported description is prograde and possibly not a remnant. The answer is D.
